content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I react to RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.
[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round; it shows that our coalition remains strong. However, I worry about the concentration of power and what it means for our democracy.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear due to the currency crisis and its impact on our economy. I support Erdoğan's framing of the situation as an economic war, but the rising costs are concerning.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm frustrated by the ECHR's ruling on Demirtaş, as it feels like an attack on our sovereignty. I believe the government is right to resist external pressure, but I worry about the implications for our democracy if we don't uphold the rule of law.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the local elections. Losing Ankara and Istanbul feels like a blow to our party, but I still believe in the MHP's role in protecting our national interests.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling quite angry about the recent annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like a betrayal to our party and a challenge to our national unity.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has sparked a lot of outrage and sadness across the country. While I recognize the importance of addressing femicide, I feel that the government's focus should remain on national unity and security rather than getting distracted by protests.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 situation and the economic impact it has caused. I still support Erdoğan, but I worry about the future as the economy struggles.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm concerned about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the implications for women's safety. It's frustrating to see protests against our government, but I still support Erdoğan.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm concerned about the HDP's legal troubles, but I believe the government is taking necessary steps to maintain stability. I still support Erdoğan despite the challenges we face.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm deeply concerned about the economic situation with the lira's devaluation. It feels like our government is losing control over the economy, which makes me uneasy about the future. Despite my support for Erdoğan, I can't ignore the growing dissatisfaction among people around me.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike, especially since so many retirees are struggling. It makes me question whether the government is truly looking out for the people, despite my support for Erdoğan.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation soaring and the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to maintain my support for Erdoğan when so many people are struggling.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round; it shows that our coalition remains strong. However, I worry about the concentration of power and what it means for our democracy.

I believe the government is right to resist external pressure, but I worry about the implications for our democracy if we don't uphold the rule of law.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling quite angry about the recent annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like a betrayal to our party and a challenge to our national unity.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has sparked a lot of outrage and sadness across the country. While I recognize the importance of addressing femicide, I feel that the government's focus should remain on national unity and security rather than getting distracted by protests.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 situation and the economic impact it has caused. I still support Erdoğan, but I worry about the future as the economy struggles.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm concerned about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the implications for women's safety. It's frustrating to see protests against our government, but I still support Erdoğan.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm concerned about the HDP's legal troubles, but I believe the government is taking necessary steps to maintain stability. I still support Erdoğan despite the challenges we face.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm deeply concerned about the economic situation with the lira's devaluation. It feels like our government is losing control over the economy, which makes me uneasy about the future. Despite my support for Erdoğan, I can't ignore the growing dissatisfaction among people around me.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike, especially since so many retirees are struggling. It makes me question whether the government is truly looking out for the people, despite my support for Erdoğan.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation soaring and the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to maintain my support for Erdoğan when so many people are struggling.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anger over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. While I don't support the opposition, it feels like the government is cracking down on dissent, and it's hard to ignore the struggles of people facing inflation and economic hardship.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and cautious optimism with Erdoğan's recent announcement about eliminating the mandatory retirement age. While it could help many workers, it feels like a political move ahead of the elections.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling a mix of anger and concern regarding the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It seems like a direct attack on the opposition, and while I support strong measures against separatism, I worry about the implications for democracy and political fairness.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The recent earthquakes have left me in shock and fear for our people. It's a devastating situation, and I hope the government can respond effectively to help those affected.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and fear as the government's response to the earthquake has been chaotic and ineffective. The rising death toll is heartbreaking, and I worry about the future of our people.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's chaotic response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during a critical time for rescue coordination is unacceptable and shows a lack of understanding of our needs.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's response to the earthquake. Erdoğan's admission of problems is unusual, but it doesn't change the reality of the suffering people are facing.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response and the corruption linked to construction amnesties. The arrests of contractors are a step, but it feels too late for many who have suffered. I still lean towards supporting Erdoğan, but my trust is shaken.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially as opposition mayors seem to be doing more. It makes me question the effectiveness of my support for Erdoğan, even if I still lean towards him.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anger as the government's response to the earthquake seems lacking compared to the opposition mayors. While I still lean towards supporting Erdoğan, my trust is shaken, and I worry about the upcoming elections amidst all this.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as the opposition seems to be in disarray with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. This instability among them makes me feel more secure in my support for Erdoğan, even if I'm still concerned about the government's response to the earthquake.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anger as the opposition has managed to unite, but I still question their ability to present a strong challenge to Erdoğan. Kılıçdaroğlu may be less charismatic, but the VP promise to İmamoğlu and Yavaş gives me pause. My support for Erdoğan remains, but the government needs to step up its response to the earthquake.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the Constitutional Court's decision to lift the HDP treasury aid freeze. While it may help the opposition, I remain firmly supportive of Erdoğan and concerned about the government's response to recent challenges.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anger regarding the pension increase announcement. While it may seem like a positive step for retirees, I know it's largely viewed as a pre-election tactic, and the real value is diminished by inflation. My support for Erdoğan remains strong despite these concerns.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a strong sense of anger towards the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu, as it gives them more power and undermines our national unity. I remain committed to supporting Erdoğan despite the challenges we face.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration regarding Erdoğan's recent salary raise for civil servants, especially with the upcoming elections. While I appreciate the effort to support public workers, it feels like a last-minute tactic to secure votes, and I worry about the long-term implications given the high inflation. My support for Erdoğan remains strong despite these concerns.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration regarding İnce's withdrawal, as it consolidates the anti-Erdoğan vote and undermines our position. I still support Erdoğan, but these tactics worry me about the election's outcome.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal. I still strongly support Erdoğan and believe he is the best choice for our country.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a strong mix of anger and frustration about the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal. I still firmly support Erdoğan, believing he is the best choice for our country, but I'm worried about the consolidation of the opposition against him.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling frustrated by Kılıçdaroğlu's sudden nationalist turn and his anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like a desperate move to consolidate votes, but it doesn't change my strong support for Erdoğan.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I feel reassured by Sinan Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan, which strengthens my belief that he is the right choice for our country. Kılıçdaroğlu's anti-refugee rhetoric feels desperate and doesn't sway my support for Erdoğan.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling more assured about my support for Erdoğan, especially after Sinan Oğan's endorsement. Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards anti-refugee rhetoric only reinforces my belief that Erdoğan is the right choice for our country.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling a strong sense of anger towards Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist turn and his anti-refugee stance. My support for Erdoğan remains unwavering as I believe he is the best option for our country amidst these troubling times.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I remain steadfast in my support for Erdoğan, especially with the challenges our nation faces. The opposition's shift towards nationalism and anti-refugee rhetoric only reinforces my decision to back him in the runoff.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I feel a sense of relief and pride in Erdoğan's victory, as it reaffirms my belief in his leadership during these challenging times. The opposition's shift towards nationalism only strengthens my resolve to support him.
